title: COMMUNICATIONS in Persia (post, telegraph, telephone, press, radio, television) — Encyclopaedia Iranica
source_org: Encyclopaedia Iranica (Encyclopaedia Iranica Foundation), article by Annabelle Sreberny-Mohammadi and Ali Mohammadi
time_coverage: 1858-1990 (telegraph from 1858; telephone from 1914; radio from 1924; TV from 1958; series data points concentrated at 1913/1914, 1953, 1965, 1975/76, 1979, 1988/89)
frequency: one-off reference article with embedded snapshot statistics at irregular multi-year intervals, all traceable to Iran Statistical Yearbook (Sal-nama-ye amari-e keshvar) editions and UNESCO reports
units: described per-file/per-row; counts, km, percent, persons
license_terms: Encyclopaedia Iranica — academic reference, cite author/article per their citation guidance
notes: This single article is the richest source found in this entire hunt: it directly cites the Iran Statistical Yearbook (Sal-nama-ye amari-e keshvar) for 1953, 1965/66, 1976 (Imperial calendar year 2535), and 1988/89 editions, giving genuine PRE-1990 official Iranian government statistics for telegraph offices, telephone offices/subscribers, radio-set ownership, TV audience reach, and postal volumes -- exactly the WDI gap this hunt targeted (WDI's IT.MLT.MAIN 'Fixed telephone subscriptions' only starts at 1960 with a flat 116,417 figure repeated 1960-1962, and has NO telegraph, radio, or TV ownership series at all, pre- or post-1990). Retrieved via interactive browser tool (get_page_text) since iranicaonline.org blocks curl/WebFetch with a Cloudflare challenge (HTTP 403), consistent with every other iranicaonline.org retrieval in this database. Four clean CSVs were built from the article's prose-embedded numbers: telegraph_offices.csv, telephone_series.csv, radio_tv_series.csv, postal_service_series.csv (postal kept here since Iran's post/telegraph/telephone were administered by a single ministry historically). A fifth extract, press_periodicals_series.csv, was placed in the sibling iran-media-history/iranica-press-newspapers/ folder instead (newspaper/periodical counts + literacy rates) since it belongs to that source slug's topic scope -- see SOURCE_NOTE.txt there, which points back to this manifest rather than duplicating the article text. All numbers transcribed exactly as printed in the source article prose; nothing interpolated, estimated, or fabricated. Where the source's own citation for a figure was ambiguous (e.g. UNESCO 'late 1980s' figure lacking an exact year), the CSV row's source column states this explicitly rather than inventing a precise date.

failures.method: curl (not separately attempted; domain confirmed Cloudflare-blocked by multiple prior retrievals in this session and in pre-existing data/raw/encyclopaedia-iranica manifests)
failures.result: not attempted, known-blocked domain, used browser tool directly
